# Data and Functions {#sec-data}

Here we load the libraries, source the helper functions, and read all required data.

```{r}
#| label: libraries-and-functions
#| cache: false # to clear data

# remotes::install_github("meta-analyses/drpa")
library(drpa)
library(tidyverse) # ggplot2, dplyr, tidyr, readr, stringr
# ── Helper functions (RunLifeTable, assignPA_RRs, calculate_pif) ───────────────
source("functions.R")

# ── Output directory ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
dir.create("outputs/lecture_5_6", recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
```

## MSLT data (Colombia) {#sec-mslt-data}

The MSLT data file contains one row per age (0–100) and sex, with
all-cause mortality rates (`mx`), disability weights (`pyld_rate`),
population size, and disease-specific parameters for Colombia. All rates
are derived from GBD 2019 Colombia estimates.

```{r}
#| label: load-mslt

mslt_general <- read_csv("data/lecture_5_6/mslt_colombia.csv") 

## change to population for Bogota, file has population for Colombia. 

population_bogota <- read_csv("data/lecture_5_6/population_bogota.csv") %>% 
  mutate(age = min_age + 2)

# Replace population in mslt_general with Bogotá population
mslt_general <- mslt_general %>%
  dplyr::select(-population) %>%
  left_join(
    population_bogota %>% dplyr::select(sex, age, population), 
    by = c("sex", "age"))

glimpse(mslt_general)
```

## Bogotá population data {#sec-bogota-data}

This file contains the Bogotá study population with marginal MET-hours baseline and pedestrian scenario created in lecture 3 and saved in lecture_5_6 data. 

```{r}
#| label: load-bogota

io <- readRDS("data/lecture_3/ithim_io/io.rds")
base_pop <- io$bogota$base_pop
travel_mmets <- read_csv("data/lecture_5_6/mmets_bogota.csv")

bogota_mmets <- travel_mmets %>%
  left_join(base_pop %>% dplyr::select(participant_id, age, sex, age_cat),
            by = "participant_id") %>%
  dplyr::select(participant_id, baseline, sc_ped, age, sex, age_cat)

bogota_mmets_long <- bogota_mmets %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(baseline, sc_ped), names_to = "scenario", values_to = "total_mmets")

bogota_mmets_long %>%
  group_by(sex, scenario) %>%
  summarise(
    n      = dplyr::n(),
    min    = round(min(total_mmets,     na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    q1     = round(quantile(total_mmets, 0.25,  na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    median = round(median(total_mmets,  na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    mean   = round(mean(total_mmets,    na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    q3     = round(quantile(total_mmets, 0.75,  na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    max    = round(max(total_mmets,     na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Life Tables {#sec-lifetable}

A **cohort life table** tracks a group of people born (or entering an
age) at the same time and follows them as they age and die. Each row
represents one year of age.

| Variable    | Meaning                                                      |
|----------------|--------------------------------------------------------|
| `mx`        | All-cause mortality *rate* at age x (deaths per person-year) |
| `qx`        | Probability of dying between age x and x+1                   |
| `lx`        | Number of survivors at age x                                 |
| `dx`        | Number who die between age x and x+1                         |
| `Lx`        | Person-years lived by the cohort between age x and x+1       |
| `ex`        | Remaining life expectancy at age x                           |
| `pyld_rate` | All-cause disability weight (proportion of health lost)      |
| `Lwx`       | Health-adjusted person-years lived (Lx × (1 − pyld_rate))    |
| `ewx`       | Health-adjusted life expectancy at age x                     |

The key relationships are:

$$q_x = 1 - e^{-m_x}$$

$$L_x = \frac{l_x + l_{x+1}}{2}$$

$$e_x = \frac{\sum_{t=x}^{100} L_t}{l_x}$$

## Reference life table (no trend) {#sec-reference}

We work with **50–54-year-old females** (mid-age = 52) throughout.
Change `in_sex` and `in_mid_age` to explore other cohorts.

Available mid-ages: 17, 22, 27, 32, 37, 42, 47, **52**, 57, 62, 67, 72,
77, 82, 87, 92, 97

```{r}
#| label: reference-life-table

lt_ref <- RunLifeTable(
  in_data    = mslt_general,
  in_sex     = "female",
  in_mid_age = 37,
  mx_trend   = NA,   # NA = no trend, use static mx
  scenario   = 0     # 0 = no PIF-based reduction
) %>%
  mutate(label = "Reference (no trend)")

write_csv(lt_ref, "outputs/lecture_5_6/life_table_reference.csv")

lt_ref %>%
  dplyr::select(age, mx, qx, lx, dx, Lx, ex, Lwx, ewx) %>%
  head(10)
```

```{r}
#| label: inspect-lt

cat(sprintf("Remaining life expectancy at age 37 (female): %.2f years\n",
            lt_ref$ex[1]))
cat(sprintf("Health-adjusted life expectancy at age 37:    %.2f years\n",
            lt_ref$ewx[1]))
cat(sprintf("\nTotal person-years lived by cohort (sum Lx):  %s\n",
            format(round(sum(lt_ref$Lx)), big.mark = ",")))
cat(sprintf("Total health-adjusted life years  (sum Lwx):  %s\n",
            format(round(sum(lt_ref$Lwx)), big.mark = ",")))
```

```{r}
#| label: plot-reference
#| fig-cap: "Reference life table for females cohort aged 35-39. Left: all-cause mortality rate rising with age. Right: cohort survivors declining over time."

p1 <- ggplot(lt_ref, aes(x = age, y = mx)) +
  geom_line(colour = "#2563EB", linewidth = 1) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(37, 100), breaks = seq(37, 100, 10),
                     expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(title = "All-cause mortality rate — females cohort aged 35-39",
       x = "Age", y = "Mortality rate (mx)") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
        axis.text  = element_text(size = 11),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 11))

p2 <- ggplot(lt_ref, aes(x = age, y = lx / 1e6)) +
  geom_line(colour = "#0D9488", linewidth = 1) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(37, 100), breaks = seq(37, 100, 10),
                     expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(title = "Survivors — females cohort aged 35-39",
       x = "Age", y = "Survivors (millions)") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
        axis.text  = element_text(size = 11),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 11))

gridExtra::grid.arrange(p1, p2, ncol = 2)
```

## What if mortality improves over time? {#sec-trend}

The reference life table above uses **static** mortality rates — the
same `mx` at every age, regardless of which calendar year the cohort
reaches that age. This is a simplification. In reality, mortality rates
change over time as healthcare, living standards, and disease management
improve (or worsen).

### How the trend is modelled

The `mx_trend` argument applies an **annual percentage change** that
**compounds** across simulation years. Because each row of the life
table represents the cohort one year older — and therefore one calendar
year further into the future — the multiplier accumulates:

$$m_x^{\text{projected}} = m_x \times \left(1 - \frac{\text{trend}}{100}\right)^{t-1}$$

where $t = 1$ at cohort entry (no change), $t = 2$ one year later, and
so on.

### Running the life table with a 0.5% annual trend

```{r}
#| label: lt-with-trend

lt_trend <- RunLifeTable(
  in_data    = mslt_general,
  in_sex     = "female",
  in_mid_age = 37,
  mx_trend   = 0.5, # 0.5% annual reduction in mx, compounding each year
  mx_trend_years = 10,
  scenario   = 0
) %>%
  mutate(label = "With trend (−0.5%/yr)")

write_csv(lt_trend, "outputs/lecture_5_6/life_table_trend.csv")
```

### Comparing reference vs trend

```{r}
#| label: compare-trend

gain_ly_trend  <- sum(lt_trend$Lx)  - sum(lt_ref$Lx)
gain_hly_trend <- sum(lt_trend$Lwx) - sum(lt_ref$Lwx)
gain_ex_trend  <- (lt_trend$ex[1]   - lt_ref$ex[1]) * 365

cat(sprintf("Difference in person-years lived (Lx):           %s\n",
            format(round(gain_ly_trend),  big.mark = ",")))
cat(sprintf("Difference in health-adjusted life years (Lwx):  %s\n",
            format(round(gain_hly_trend), big.mark = ",")))
cat(sprintf("Difference in life expectancy:                   %.1f days (%.2f years)\n",
            gain_ex_trend, gain_ex_trend / 365))
```

```{r}
#| label: plot-trend-comparison
#| fig-cap: "Effect of a 0.5% annual mortality reduction on the mortality rate trajectory (left) and cumulative person-years lived (right). The gap between curves widens with age as the compounding effect accumulates."

lt_compare_trend <- bind_rows(lt_ref, lt_trend)

p_mx <- ggplot(lt_compare_trend, aes(x = age, y = mx, colour = label)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("Reference (no trend)"    = "#0D9488",
                                 "With trend (−0.5%/yr)"   = "#7C3AED")) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(37, 100), breaks = seq(37, 100, 10),
                     expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(title = "Mortality rate: reference vs 0.5% annual trend",
       x = "Age", y = "Mortality rate (mx)", colour = NULL) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
        axis.text  = element_text(size = 11),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 11),
        legend.position = "bottom")

p_lx <- ggplot(lt_compare_trend, aes(x = age, y = Lx / 1e6, colour = label)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("Reference (no trend)"    = "#0D9488",
                                 "With trend (−0.5%/yr)"   = "#7C3AED")) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(37, 100), breaks = seq(52, 100, 10),
                     expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(title = "Person-years lived: reference vs 0.5% annual trend",
       x = "Age", y = "Person-years (millions)", colour = NULL) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
        axis.text  = element_text(size = 11),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 11),
        legend.position = "bottom")

gridExtra::grid.arrange(p_mx, p_lx, ncol = 2)
```

```{r}
#| label: summary-table-trend
#| echo: false

tibble(
  Scenario            = c("Reference (no trend)",
                           "With trend (−0.5%/yr)",
                           "Difference"),
  `Life expectancy`   = c(round(lt_ref$ex[1],   2),
                           round(lt_trend$ex[1], 2),
                           round(lt_trend$ex[1] - lt_ref$ex[1], 2)),
  `HA life expectancy`= c(round(lt_ref$ewx[1],   2),
                           round(lt_trend$ewx[1], 2),
                           round(lt_trend$ewx[1] - lt_ref$ewx[1], 2)),
  `Sum Lx`            = c(format(round(sum(lt_ref$Lx)),   big.mark = ","),
                           format(round(sum(lt_trend$Lx)), big.mark = ","),
                           format(round(gain_ly_trend),    big.mark = ",")),
  `Sum Lwx`           = c(format(round(sum(lt_ref$Lwx)),   big.mark = ","),
                           format(round(sum(lt_trend$Lwx)), big.mark = ","),
                           format(round(gain_hly_trend),    big.mark = ","))
) |> knitr::kable()
```

::: callout-tip
## Interpretation

A 0.5% annual compound reduction in all-cause mortality adds
approximately `r format(round(gain_ly_trend), big.mark = ",")`
person-years to this cohort over their remaining lifetime and increases
life expectancy by about `r round(gain_ex_trend)` days. 
:::

::: callout-note
## Extending this

`mx_trend` can also be **negative** to model worsening mortality (e.g.
`mx_trend = -1` for a 1% annual increase). 
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Scenario 1 — Direct Mortality Reduction (5%) {#sec-scenario1}

**Question:** What is the gain in life years and life expectancy if
all-cause mortality is reduced by 5% across all ages?

The `scenario` argument applies a one-off proportional reduction to `mx`
at every age:

$$m_x^{\text{scenario}} = m_x \times (1 - \text{scenario})$$

This is distinct from `mx_trend`, which compounds year-on-year. The two
can be combined: `scenario` captures a structural shift (e.g. from a
policy intervention) while `mx_trend` captures background secular
change.

```{r}
#| label: scenario-mortality-reduction

lt_sc5 <- RunLifeTable(
  in_data    = mslt_general,
  in_sex     = "female",
  in_mid_age = 37,
  mx_trend   = NA,
  scenario   = 0.05      # 5% one-off reduction at all ages
) %>%
  mutate(label = "5% mortality reduction")

write_csv(lt_sc5, "outputs/lecture_5_6/life_table_scenario_5pct.csv")
```

## Comparing reference vs scenario

```{r}
#| label: compare-scenarios

gain_ly  <- sum(lt_sc5$Lx)  - sum(lt_ref$Lx)
gain_hly <- sum(lt_sc5$Lwx) - sum(lt_ref$Lwx)
gain_ex  <- (lt_sc5$ex[1]   - lt_ref$ex[1]) * 365

cat(sprintf("Gain in person-years lived (Lx):           %s\n",
            format(round(gain_ly),  big.mark = ",")))
cat(sprintf("Gain in health-adjusted life years (Lwx):  %s\n",
            format(round(gain_hly), big.mark = ",")))
cat(sprintf("Gain in life expectancy:                   %.1f days (%.2f years)\n",
            gain_ex, gain_ex / 365))
```

```{r}
#| label: plot-scenario-comparison
#| fig-cap: "5% one-off mortality reduction vs reference. Unlike the trend, the gap between curves is constant across ages — the proportional reduction is uniform."

lt_compare <- bind_rows(lt_ref, lt_sc5)

p3 <- ggplot(lt_compare, aes(x = age, y = mx, colour = label)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("Reference (no trend)"   = "#2563EB",
                                 "5% mortality reduction" = "#DC2626")) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(37, 100), breaks = seq(37, 100, 10),
                     expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(title = "Mortality rate: reference vs 5% reduction",
       x = "Age", y = "Mortality rate (mx)", colour = NULL) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
        axis.text  = element_text(size = 11),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 11),
        legend.position = "bottom")

p4 <- ggplot(lt_compare, aes(x = age, y = Lx / 1e6, colour = label)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("Reference (no trend)"   = "#2563EB",
                                 "5% mortality reduction" = "#DC2626")) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(37, 100), breaks = seq(52, 100, 10),
                     expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(title = "Person-years lived: reference vs 5% reduction",
       x = "Age", y = "Person-years (millions)", colour = NULL) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
        axis.text  = element_text(size = 11),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 11),
        legend.position = "bottom")

gridExtra::grid.arrange(p3, p4, ncol = 2)
```

## Summary table — reference, trend, and scenario

```{r}
#| label: summary-table-scenario1
#| echo: false

tibble(
  Scenario            = c("Reference (no trend)",
                           "With trend (−0.5%/yr)",
                           "5% mortality reduction",
                           "Difference (trend vs ref)",
                           "Difference (5% sc vs ref)"),
  `Life expectancy`   = c(round(lt_ref$ex[1],   2),
                           round(lt_trend$ex[1], 2),
                           round(lt_sc5$ex[1],   2),
                           round(lt_trend$ex[1] - lt_ref$ex[1], 2),
                           round(lt_sc5$ex[1]   - lt_ref$ex[1], 2)),
  `HA life expectancy`= c(round(lt_ref$ewx[1],   2),
                           round(lt_trend$ewx[1], 2),
                           round(lt_sc5$ewx[1],   2),
                           round(lt_trend$ewx[1] - lt_ref$ewx[1], 2),
                           round(lt_sc5$ewx[1]   - lt_ref$ewx[1], 2)),
  `Sum Lx`            = c(format(round(sum(lt_ref$Lx)),   big.mark = ","),
                           format(round(sum(lt_trend$Lx)), big.mark = ","),
                           format(round(sum(lt_sc5$Lx)),   big.mark = ","),
                           format(round(gain_ly_trend),    big.mark = ","),
                           format(round(gain_ly),          big.mark = ","))
) |> knitr::kable()
```

::: callout-tip
## Scenario vs trend — a key teaching point

Notice that in simulation using a cohort perspective (in this
implementation include trends) is preferred since the aim is to simulate
what would happen in the future under a counterfactual.
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

---

## Example 2 — Physical Activity Increase (Bogotá) {#sec-scenario2}

**Question:** What is the gain in life years from a population-level
increase in physical activity (pedestrian scenario) for the Bogotá
population?

The pathway is:

$$\text{PA distribution} \xrightarrow{\text{dose-response}} \text{RR by individual} \xrightarrow{\text{PIF}} \Delta m_x \xrightarrow{\text{life table}} \Delta \text{life years}$$

## Step 1 — Assign individual-level RRs {#sec-rrs}

```{r}
#| label: compute-rrs

diseases <- "all-cause-mortality"

bogota_ref  <- bogota_mmets_long %>%
  filter(scenario == "baseline") %>%
  rename(mmet = total_mmets) %>%
  assignPA_RRs(., diseases = diseases, mmet_col = "mmet")

bogota_scen <- bogota_mmets_long %>%
  filter(scenario == "sc_ped") %>%
  rename(mmet = total_mmets) %>%
  assignPA_RRs(., diseases = diseases, mmet_col = "mmet")

# Sanity check — RR should be <= 1
cat("Reference RR summary:\n")
summary(bogota_ref$`rr_PHYSICAL_ACTIVITY_all-cause-mortality`)
cat("\nScenario RR summary (should be slightly lower):\n")
summary(bogota_scen$`rr_PHYSICAL_ACTIVITY_all-cause-mortality`)
```

## Step 2 — Calculate PIFs {#sec-pifs}

$$\text{PIF} = \frac{\sum \text{RR}(\text{ref}) - \sum \text{RR}(\text{scen})}{\sum \text{RR}(\text{ref})}$$

```{r}
#| label: compute-pifs

pif_results <- calculate_pif(pop_ref = bogota_ref, pop_scen = bogota_scen)
pifs        <- pif_results$pif_table

print(pifs, n = Inf)
```

## Step 3 — Run life table with PIF-modified mortality {#sec-lt-pa}

$$m_x^{\text{PA scenario}} = m_x \times (1 - \text{PIF})$$

```{r}
#| label: life-table-pa-scenario

lt_pa <- RunLifeTable(
  in_data    = mslt_general,
  in_sex     = "female",
  in_mid_age = 37,
  mx_trend   = NA,
  scenario   = pifs
) %>%
  mutate(label = "Walking sceneario")

write_csv(lt_pa, "outputs/lecture_5_6/life_table_pa_scenario.csv")
```

## Step 5 — Compare reference vs pedestrian scenario {#sec-compare-pa}

```{r}
#| label: compare-pa-scenario

gain_ly_pa  <- sum(lt_pa$Lx)  - sum(lt_ref$Lx)
gain_hly_pa <- sum(lt_pa$Lwx) - sum(lt_ref$Lwx)
gain_ex_pa  <- (lt_pa$ex[1]   - lt_ref$ex[1]) * 365

cat(sprintf("Gain in person-years lived (Lx):          %s\n",
            format(round(gain_ly_pa),  big.mark = ",")))
cat(sprintf("Gain in health-adjusted life years (Lwx): %s\n",
            format(round(gain_hly_pa), big.mark = ",")))
cat(sprintf("Gain in life expectancy:                  %.1f days\n",
            gain_ex_pa))
```

```{r}
#| label: plot-pa-scenario
#| fig-cap: "PA scenario vs reference. The PIF reduces mx proportionally at all ages, similar in shape to the 5% one-off reduction but typically smaller in magnitude."

lt_compare_pa <- bind_rows(lt_ref, lt_pa)

p5 <- ggplot(lt_compare_pa, aes(x = age, y = mx, colour = label)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("Reference (no trend)"    = "#2563EB",
                                 "PA scenario (+2 mMETs)"  = "#D97706")) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(37, 100), breaks = seq(37, 100, 10),
                     expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(title = "Mortality rate: reference vs PA scenario",
       x = "Age", y = "Mortality rate (mx)", colour = NULL) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
        axis.text  = element_text(size = 11),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 11),
        legend.position = "bottom")

p6 <- ggplot(lt_compare_pa, aes(x = age, y = Lx / 1e6, colour = label)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("Reference (no trend)"    = "#2563EB",
                                 "PA scenario (+2 mMETs)"  = "#D97706")) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(37, 100), breaks = seq(37, 100, 10),
                     expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(title = "Person-years lived: reference vs PA scenario",
       x = "Age", y = "Person-years (millions)", colour = NULL) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
        axis.text  = element_text(size = 11),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 11),
        legend.position = "bottom")

gridExtra::grid.arrange(p5, p6, ncol = 2)
```

## Summary table — all scenarios {#sec-summary}

```{r}
#| label: final-summary-table
#| echo: false

tibble(
  Scenario             = c("Reference (no trend)",
                            "With trend (−0.5%/yr)",
                            "5% mortality reduction",
                            "Pedestrian scenario"),
  `mx_trend`           = c("NA", "0.5", "NA", "NA"),
  `scenario (PIF)`     = c("0", "0", "5%", "age-specific"),
  `Life expectancy`    = c(round(lt_ref$ex[1],   2),
                            round(lt_trend$ex[1], 2),
                            round(lt_sc5$ex[1],   2),
                            round(lt_pa$ex[1],    2)),
  `HA life expectancy` = c(round(lt_ref$ewx[1],   2),
                            round(lt_trend$ewx[1], 2),
                            round(lt_sc5$ewx[1],   2),
                            round(lt_pa$ewx[1],    2)),
  `Gain Lx`            = c("—",
                            format(round(gain_ly_trend), big.mark = ","),
                            format(round(gain_ly),       big.mark = ","),
                            format(round(gain_ly_pa),    big.mark = ",")),
  `Gain Lwx`           = c("—",
                            format(round(gain_hly_trend), big.mark = ","),
                            format(round(gain_hly),       big.mark = ","),
                            format(round(gain_hly_pa),    big.mark = ","))
) |> knitr::kable()
```

**Age-specific PIFs used for PA scenario (female):**

```{r}
#| label: age-specific-pif-table
#| echo: false

pifs %>%
  filter(sex == "female") %>%
  dplyr::select(age_cat, pif) %>%
  mutate(pif_pct = paste0(round(pif * 100, 2), "%")) %>%
  dplyr::select(-pif) %>%
  knitr::kable(col.names = c("Age category", "PIF"))
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Exercise — extend to multiple cohorts {#sec-exercise}

Try running the full pipeline for **males cohort aged 50-54** and for
**females cohort aged 35-39**, both with and without the 0.5% trend.
What happens to the absolute life-year gains? Why does the trend matter
more for younger cohorts?

```{r}
#| label: exercise-multiple-cohorts
#| eval: false

for (sex_i in c("female", "male")) {
  for (age_i in c(37, 52)) {
    
    pop_start <- mslt_general %>% 
      filter(age == age_i, sex == sex_i) %>% 
      pull(population) %>% unique()
    
    lt_ref    <- RunLifeTable(mslt_general, sex_i, age_i,
                               mx_trend = 0.5, scenario = 0)
    lt_sc5    <- RunLifeTable(mslt_general, sex_i, age_i,
                               mx_trend = 0.5, scenario = 0.05)
    lt_pa     <- RunLifeTable(mslt_general, sex_i, age_i,
                               mx_trend = 0.5, scenario = pifs)

    gain_sc5   <- (sum(lt_sc5$Lx)   - sum(lt_ref$Lx)) / pop_start * 1000
    gain_pa    <- (sum(lt_pa$Lx)    - sum(lt_ref$Lx)) / pop_start * 1000
    
    cat(sprintf(
      "%s aged %d (%.0f) | 5%% mx reduction: %s LY/1000 | PA scenario: %s LY/1000\n",
      sex_i, age_i, pop_start,
      format(round(gain_sc5, 1), nsmall = 1),
      format(round(gain_pa,  1), nsmall = 1)
    ))
  }
}
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Disease Tables — Three-State Model {#sec-disease}

So far we have worked with a **two-state life table**: alive → dead,
driven by all-cause mortality. The multi-state life table extends this
by adding a **disease layer** for each condition of interest. Each
disease follows the three-state process from Barendregt et al. (1998):

``` text
Healthy (Sx) --incidence--> Diseased (Cx) --case fatality--> Dead from disease (Dx)
              <--remission--
```

At cohort entry, some individuals may already have the disease based on 
prevalence data from GBD. The model initializes with $C_x$ people diseased 
and $S_x = 1000 - C_x$ healthy, then the constraint $S_x + C_x + D_x = 1{,}000$ 
holds at every subsequent age.

`RunDisease()` implements this process. It accepts the same `inc_trend`
and `cf_trend` scalar parameters as `RunLifeTable()` accepts `mx_trend`
— a compounding annual percentage change applied across simulation
years:

$$\text{incidence}[t] = \text{incidence}[1] \times \left(1 - \frac{\text{inc\_trend}}{100}\right)^{t-1}$$

## Disease table variables {#sec-dis-vars}

| Variable                | Meaning                                |
|-------------------------|----------------------------------------|
| `Sx`                    | Healthy people at age x (out of 1,000) |
| `Cx`                    | People with the disease at age x       |
| `Dx`                    | Dead from disease at age x             |
| `px`                    | Prevalence = Cx / (Sx + Cx)            |
| `mx`                    | Disease-specific mortality rate        |
| `incidence_disease`     | Rate of new disease onset              |
| `case_fatality_disease` | Rate of dying given diseased           |
| `remission`             | Rate of recovering from disease        |
| `dw`                    | Disability weight for this disease     |
| `PYx`                   | Person-years at risk                   |

## Reference disease tables (no trends) {#sec-dis-ref}

We run reference disease tables for all three Colombia diseases for the
same cohort as the life table (females, cohort aged 35-39).

```{r}
#| label: disease-reference

# ── IHD reference ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
dis_ref_ishd <- RunDisease(
  in_data      = mslt_general,
  in_sex       = "female",
  in_mid_age   = 37,
  in_disease   = "ishd",
  inc_trend    = NA,    # static rates
  cf_trend     = NA,
  scenario_inc = 0,
  scenario_cf  = 0
) %>% mutate(label = "Reference")

# ── COPD reference ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
dis_ref_copd <- RunDisease(
  in_data      = mslt_general,
  in_sex       = "female",
  in_mid_age   = 37,
  in_disease   = "copd",
  inc_trend    = NA,
  cf_trend     = NA,
  scenario_inc = 0,
  scenario_cf  = 0
) %>% mutate(label = "Reference")

# ── Type 2 diabetes reference ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
dis_ref_dmt2 <- RunDisease(
  in_data      = mslt_general,
  in_sex       = "female",
  in_mid_age   = 37,
  in_disease   = "dmt2",
  inc_trend    = NA,
  cf_trend     = NA,
  scenario_inc = 0,
  scenario_cf  = 0
) %>% mutate(label = "Reference")

# Inspect IHD first 8 rows
dis_ref_ishd %>%
  dplyr::select(age, incidence_disease, case_fatality_disease, Sx, Cx, Dx,Tx, px, mx) %>%
  head(8)
```

## Adding an incidence trend {#sec-dis-trend}

Exactly as `mx_trend` in `RunLifeTable()`, `inc_trend = 0.5` applies a
0.5% annual compounding reduction in incidence across simulation years.
The formula is:

$$\text{incidence}[t] = \text{incidence}[1] \times (0.995)^{t-1}$$

```{r}
#| label: disease-trend

dis_trend_ishd <- RunDisease(
  in_data      = mslt_general,
  in_sex       = "female",
  in_mid_age   = 37,
  in_disease   = "ishd",
  inc_trend    = 0.5,   # 0.5% annual decline in incidence, compounding
  cf_trend     = NA,
  inc_trend_years = 10,
  scenario_inc = 0,
  scenario_cf  = 0
) %>% mutate(label = "With trend (inc −0.5%/yr)")

# Compare IHD prevalence reference vs trend
dis_ishd_compare <- bind_rows(dis_ref_ishd, dis_trend_ishd)

ggplot(dis_ishd_compare, aes(x = age, y = incidence_disease, colour = label)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("Reference" = "#DC2626",
                                 "With trend (inc −0.5%/yr)" = "#7C3AED")) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(37, 99), breaks = seq(37, 99, 10), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(title = "IHD Incidence: reference vs 0.5% annual incidence decline",
       x = "Age", y = "Incidence", colour = NULL) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 11), axis.title = element_text(size = 11),
        legend.position = "bottom")
```

## Example 1 — 5% reduction in incidence {#sec-dis-sc5}

**Question:** What happens to disease prevalence and disease-specific
mortality if incidence falls by 5% permanently at all ages?

```{r}
#| label: disease-scenario-5pct

# Run 5% scenario for all three diseases
dis_sc5_ishd <- RunDisease(
  in_data      = mslt_general,
  in_sex       = "female",
  in_mid_age   = 37,
  in_disease   = "ishd",
  inc_trend    = NA,
  cf_trend     = NA,
  scenario_inc = 0.05,    # 5% one-off reduction in incidence
  scenario_cf  = 0
) %>% mutate(label = "5% incidence reduction")

dis_sc5_copd <- RunDisease(
  in_data      = mslt_general,
  in_sex       = "female",
  in_mid_age   = 37,
  in_disease   = "copd",
  inc_trend    = NA,
  cf_trend     = NA,
  scenario_inc = 0.05,
  scenario_cf  = 0
) %>% mutate(label = "5% incidence reduction")

dis_sc5_dmt2 <- RunDisease(
  in_data      = mslt_general,
  in_sex       = "female",
  in_mid_age   = 37,
  in_disease   = "dmt2",
  inc_trend    = NA,
  cf_trend     = NA,
  scenario_inc = 0.05,
  scenario_cf  = 0
) %>% mutate(label = "5% incidence reduction")
```


```{r}
#| label: plot-disease-scenario-5pct
#| fig-cap: "IHD, COPD and DMT2 incidence rates: reference vs 5% one-off reduction. The gap between curves is constant across ages (unlike a trend, which accumulates)."

dis_all_sc5 <- bind_rows(
  dis_ref_ishd, dis_sc5_ishd,
  dis_ref_copd, dis_sc5_copd,
  dis_ref_dmt2, dis_sc5_dmt2
)

ggplot(dis_all_sc5, aes(x = age, y = incidence_disease, colour = disease, linetype = label)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(37, 99), breaks = seq(37, 99, 10), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("ishd" = "#DC2626", "copd" = "#D97706", "dmt2" = "#7C3AED")) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c("Reference" = "solid", "5% incidence reduction" = "dashed")) +
  labs(title = "Incidence rate: reference vs 5% reduction — IHD, COPD, DMT2",
       x = "Age", y = "Incidence rate", colour = "Disease", linetype = NULL) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 11), axis.title = element_text(size = 11),
        legend.position = "bottom")

# Prevalence (px) plot
ggplot(dis_all_sc5, aes(x = age, y = px, colour = disease, linetype = label)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(37, 99), breaks = seq(37, 99, 10), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("ishd" = "#DC2626", "copd" = "#D97706", "dmt2" = "#7C3AED")) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c("Reference" = "solid", "5% incidence reduction" = "dashed")) +
  labs(title = "Prevalence: reference vs 5% reduction — IHD, COPD, DMT2",
       x = "Age", y = "Prevalence (px)", colour = "Disease", linetype = NULL) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 11), axis.title = element_text(size = 11),
        legend.position = "bottom")

# Mortality (mx) plot
ggplot(dis_all_sc5, aes(x = age, y = mx, colour = disease, linetype = label)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(37, 98), breaks = seq(37, 98, 10), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("ishd" = "#DC2626", "copd" = "#D97706", "dmt2" = "#7C3AED")) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c("Reference" = "solid", "5% incidence reduction" = "dashed")) +
  labs(title = "Mortality rate: reference vs 5% reduction — IHD, COPD, DMT2",
       x = "Age", y = "Mortality rate (mx)", colour = "Disease", linetype = NULL) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 11), axis.title = element_text(size = 11),
        legend.position = "bottom")
```

::: callout-tip
## Key teaching point — disease scenario vs life table scenario

`scenario_inc = 0.05` in `RunDisease()` reduces incidence by 5% at every
age. The full effect on life years only materialises inside
`CalculationModel()`, which propagates the disease mortality and disability reduction into the general life table. The disease table alone shows you the
epidemiological change; the MSLT shows you the life-years consequence.
:::

---

## Complete MSLT — Physical Activity and Diseases {#sec-mslt}

The full proportional multi-state life table (Barendregt et al. 1998)
combines:

1.  A **general life table** tracking total survival (from
    `RunLifeTable()`)
2.  **Disease sections** for each modelled condition (from
    `RunDisease()`)
3.  **Feedback**: disease-specific mortality from step 2 modifies total
    `mx`  in step 1 and alternatively `mx` is modified directly by the PIF 

`CalculationModel()` runs all three components simultaneously and
returns differences (scenario − reference) in life years,
health-adjusted life years, and disease-specific incidence and
mortality.

---

## Example 1 — PA scenario with disease-specific PIFs {#sec-mslt-pa}

**Question:** What is the gain in life years and health-adjusted life
years from PA increase from the Pedestrian scenario, modelled through disease-specific
incidence reductions for IHD and type 2 diabetes?

The pathway is:

$$\text{PA distribution} \xrightarrow{\text{dose-response}} \text{RR by disease} \xrightarrow{\text{PIF per disease}} \Delta\text{incidence} \xrightarrow{\text{MSLT}} \Delta\text{life years}$$

### Step 1 — Assign disease-specific RRs {#sec-mslt-rrs}

```{r}
#| label: mslt-assign-rrs

# Disease names as used by the drpa package
diseases_mslt <- c(
  "coronary-heart-disease",
  # "chronic-obstructive-pulmonary-disease", not a physical activity disease
  "diabetes",
  "all-cause-mortality"          # also included for the life table component
)

# Assign RRs for reference and counterfactual PA distributions
bogota_ref  <- bogota_mmets_long %>%
  filter(scenario == "baseline") %>%
  rename(mmet = total_mmets) %>%
  assignPA_RRs(., diseases = diseases_mslt, mmet_col = "mmet")

bogota_scen <- bogota_mmets_long %>%
  filter(scenario == "sc_ped") %>%
  rename(mmet = total_mmets) %>%
  assignPA_RRs(., diseases = diseases_mslt, mmet_col = "mmet")

# Quick sanity check — all RRs should be <= 1 (PA is protective)
rr_cols <- grep("^rr_", names(bogota_ref), value = TRUE)
cat("Reference RR ranges:\n")
for (col in rr_cols) {
  cat(sprintf("  %-60s min=%.4f  mean=%.4f\n",
              col, min(bogota_ref[[col]]), mean(bogota_ref[[col]])))
}

cat("Scenario RR ranges:\n")
for (col in rr_cols) {
  cat(sprintf("  %-60s min=%.4f  mean=%.4f\n",
              col, min(bogota_scen[[col]]), mean(bogota_scen[[col]])))
}
```

### Step 2 — Calculate disease-specific PIFs {#sec-mslt-pifs}

```{r}
#| label: mslt-pifs

pif_inc <- calculate_pif(pop_ref  = bogota_ref,
                                   pop_scen = bogota_scen)
pifs_inc        <- pif_inc$pif_table %>% rename(disease = cause)

cat("PIFs by disease for incidence, sex and age group:\n")
print(pifs_inc  %>% arrange(disease, sex, age_cat), n = Inf)
```

### Step 3 — Run the complete MSLT {#sec-mslt-run}

First, assess whether the model is working properly. It is expected that without an interventions or difference in trends the model should produce zero outputs since it compares exactly the same sets of MSLTs for the same population cohort. 

```{r}
#| label: mslt-validation

# ── Validation: run with zero scenario — all differences must be zero ─────────
output_test <- CalculationModel(
  in_data      = mslt_general,
  in_sex       = "female",
  in_mid_age   = 37,
  mx_trend     = NA,
  in_disease   = c("ishd", "copd", "dmt2"),
  inc_trend    = NA,
  cf_trend     = NA,
  scenario_inc = 0,
  scenario_cf  = 0
)

cat("Validation checks (all must be zero):\n")
cat(sprintf("  sum(Lx_diff):              %.6f\n", sum(output_test$Lx_diff)))
cat(sprintf("  sum(Lwx_diff):             %.6f\n", sum(output_test$Lwx_diff)))
cat(sprintf("  sum(inc_num_diff_ishd):    %.6f\n", sum(output_test$inc_num_diff_ishd)))
cat(sprintf("  sum(inc_num_diff_copd):    %.6f\n", sum(output_test$inc_num_diff_copd)))
cat(sprintf("  sum(inc_num_diff_dmt2):    %.6f\n", sum(output_test$inc_num_diff_dmt2)))
```
Second, assess whether the modeled prevalence and mortality aligns with the input data for the selected diseases. 

```{r}
#| label: mslt-gbd-comparison-validation

# Compare baseline (no intervention) model outputs with GBD data
# Load GBD data for comparison
gbd_data <- read_csv("data/lecture_5_6/gbd_mslt_2019_colombia.csv")

# Filter GBD data
gbd_prev <- gbd_data %>%
  filter(measure_name == "Prevalence",
         metric_name == "Rate",
         sex_name == "Female") %>%
  mutate(age = case_when(
    age_name == "95+ years" ~ 95,
    TRUE ~ as.integer(str_extract(age_name, "^\\d+"))
  )) %>%
  mutate(disease = case_match(cause_name,
    "Ischemic heart disease" ~ "ishd",
    "Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease" ~ "copd", 
    "Diabetes mellitus type 2" ~ "dmt2",
    .default = NA_character_
  )) %>%
  filter(!is.na(age), !is.na(disease), age >= 15) %>%
  dplyr::select(age, disease, gbd_px = val)

gbd_mort <- gbd_data %>%
  filter(measure_name == "Deaths",
         metric_name == "Rate",
         sex_name == "Female") %>%
  mutate(age = case_when(
    age_name == "95+ years" ~ 95,
    TRUE ~ as.integer(str_extract(age_name, "^\\d+"))
  )) %>%
  mutate(disease = case_match(cause_name,
    "Ischemic heart disease" ~ "ishd",
    "Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease" ~ "copd",
    "Diabetes mellitus type 2" ~ "dmt2",
    .default = NA_character_
  )) %>%
  filter(!is.na(age), !is.na(disease)) %>%
  dplyr::select(age, disease, gbd_mx = val)

# Run baseline disease tables for comparison
dis_ref_ishd <- RunDisease(
  in_data      = mslt_general,
  in_sex       = "female",
  in_mid_age   = 37,
  in_disease   = "ishd",
  scenario_inc = 0,
  scenario_cf  = 0
) %>% mutate(disease = "ishd")

dis_ref_copd <- RunDisease(
  in_data      = mslt_general,
  in_sex       = "female",
  in_mid_age   = 37,
  in_disease   = "copd",
  scenario_inc = 0,
  scenario_cf  = 0
) %>% mutate(disease = "copd")

dis_ref_dmt2 <- RunDisease(
  in_data      = mslt_general,
  in_sex       = "female",
  in_mid_age   = 37,
  in_disease   = "dmt2",
  scenario_inc = 0,
  scenario_cf  = 0
) %>% mutate(disease = "dmt2")

# Merge model outputs with GBD data
dis_all_ref <- bind_rows(dis_ref_ishd, dis_ref_copd, dis_ref_dmt2) %>%
  filter(age >= 37, age <= 90) %>%
  left_join(gbd_prev, by = c("age", "disease")) %>%
  left_join(gbd_mort, by = c("age", "disease")) %>%
  mutate(
    model_px = px * 100000,
    model_mx = mx * 100000
  )

# Print comparison summary
cat("\n=== Model vs GBD Comparison (baseline, no intervention) ===\n")
for (dis in c("ishd", "copd", "dmt2")) {
  d <- dis_all_ref %>% filter(disease == dis)
  cat("\n", toupper(dis), ":\n")
  cat("  Prevalence - Model range:", round(range(d$model_px, na.rm = TRUE), 1), "\n")
  cat("  Prevalence - GBD range:  ", round(range(d$gbd_px, na.rm = TRUE), 1), "\n")
  cat("  Prevalence - Correlation:", round(cor(d$model_px, d$gbd_px, use = "complete.obs"), 3), "\n")
  cat("  Mortality - Model range: ", round(range(d$model_mx, na.rm = TRUE), 1), "\n")
  cat("  Mortality - GBD range:   ", round(range(d$gbd_mx, na.rm = TRUE), 1), "\n")
  cat("  Mortality - Correlation: ", round(cor(d$model_mx, d$gbd_mx, use = "complete.obs"), 3), "\n")
}

# Plot comparison
p_px <- ggplot(dis_all_ref %>% filter(age <= 90), aes(x = age)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = model_px, colour = disease), linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(aes(y = gbd_px, colour = disease), shape = 1, size = 2, na.rm = TRUE) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(37, 95), breaks = seq(40, 90, 10)) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("ishd" = "#DC2626", "copd" = "#D97706", "dmt2" = "#7C3AED")) +
  labs(title = "Disease Prevalence: Model (line) vs GBD (points)", 
       x = "Age", y = "Prevalence (per 100,000)", colour = "Disease") +
  theme_classic() + theme(legend.position = "bottom")

p_mx <- ggplot(dis_all_ref %>% filter(age < 99), aes(x = age)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = model_mx, colour = disease), linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(aes(y = gbd_mx, colour = disease), shape = 1, size = 2, na.rm = TRUE) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(37, 95), breaks = seq(40, 90, 10)) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("ishd" = "#DC2626", "copd" = "#D97706", "dmt2" = "#7C3AED")) +
  labs(title = "Disease Mortality: Model (line) vs GBD (points)", 
       x = "Age", y = "Mortality (per 100,000)", colour = "Disease") +
  theme_classic() + theme(legend.position = "bottom")

print(p_px)
print(p_mx)
```

```{r}
#| label: mslt-pa-scenario

# Create age-specific PIF dataframes for diseases and all-cause (female)
# Note: column names must contain "inc" for scenario_inc, "cf" for scenario_cf, 
# and "mort"/"all_cause"/"pif" for scenario (mortality)
pif_ishd_by_age <- pifs_inc %>% 
  dplyr::filter(disease == "coronary_heart_disease", sex == "female") %>%
  dplyr::arrange(age_cat) %>%
  dplyr::select(age_cat, sex, pif_inc_ishd = pif)

pif_dmt2_by_age <- pifs_inc %>% 
  dplyr::filter(disease == "diabetes", sex == "female") %>%
  dplyr::arrange(age_cat) %>%
  dplyr::select(age_cat, sex, pif_inc_dmt2 = pif)

pif_acm_by_age <- pifs_inc %>% 
  dplyr::filter(disease == "all_cause_mortality", sex == "female") %>%
  dplyr::arrange(age_cat) %>%
  dplyr::select(age_cat, sex, pif_mort = pif)

# ── Scenario A: disease-specific mortality pathway (use_disease_mx = TRUE)
# Disease PIFs → reduced incidence → reduced disease mortality → reduced all-cause mx
# Using dataframes for scenario_inc (age_cat with PIFs, expanded to single ages in RunDisease)
output_mslt_dis <- CalculationModel(
  in_data        = mslt_general,
  in_sex         = "female",
  in_mid_age     = 37,
  mx_trend       = NA,
  in_disease     = c("ishd", "copd", "dmt2"),
  inc_trend      = NA,
  cf_trend       = NA,
  scenario       = 0,
  scenario_inc   = list(ishd = pif_ishd_by_age, copd = 0, dmt2 = pif_dmt2_by_age),
  scenario_cf    = 0,
  use_disease_mx = TRUE
)

# ── Scenario B: all-cause mortality pathway (use_disease_mx = FALSE)
# Same disease incidence changes, but age-specific all-cause PIF applied directly to mx
# scenario as dataframe applies age-specific PIF to all-cause mx
output_mslt_acm <- CalculationModel(
  in_data        = mslt_general,
  in_sex         = "female",
  in_mid_age     = 37,
  mx_trend       = NA,
  in_disease     = c("ishd", "copd", "dmt2"),
  inc_trend      = NA,
  cf_trend       = NA,
  scenario       = pif_acm_by_age,  # age-specific all-cause PIF as dataframe
  scenario_inc   = list(ishd = pif_ishd_by_age, copd = 0, dmt2 = pif_dmt2_by_age),
  scenario_cf    = 0,
  use_disease_mx = FALSE
)

write_csv(output_mslt_dis, "outputs/lecture_5_6/mslt_pa_disease_pifs.csv")
write_csv(output_mslt_acm, "outputs/lecture_5_6/mslt_pa_allcause_pif.csv")
```

### Step 5 — Results {#sec-mslt-results}

```{r}
#| label: mslt-results-table
#| echo: false

fmt_diff <- function(x) {
  x <- round(x)
  sign <- ifelse(x > 0, "+", "")
  paste0(sign, format(x, big.mark = ","))
}

scen_a_ly   <- sum(output_mslt_dis$Lx_diff)
scen_a_lwx  <- sum(output_mslt_dis$Lwx_diff)
scen_a_ex   <- output_mslt_dis$ex_diff[1] * 365
scen_a_ishd_cases <- sum(output_mslt_dis$inc_num_diff_ishd)
scen_a_copd_cases <- sum(output_mslt_dis$inc_num_diff_copd)
scen_a_dmt2_cases <- sum(output_mslt_dis$inc_num_diff_dmt2)
scen_a_ishd_deaths <- sum(output_mslt_dis$mx_num_diff_ishd)
scen_a_copd_deaths <- sum(output_mslt_dis$mx_num_diff_copd)
scen_a_dmt2_deaths <- sum(output_mslt_dis$mx_num_diff_dmt2)

scen_b_ly   <- sum(output_mslt_acm$Lx_diff)
scen_b_lwx  <- sum(output_mslt_acm$Lwx_diff)
scen_b_ex   <- output_mslt_acm$ex_diff[1] * 365
scen_b_ishd_cases <- sum(output_mslt_acm$inc_num_diff_ishd)
scen_b_copd_cases <- sum(output_mslt_acm$inc_num_diff_copd)
scen_b_dmt2_cases <- sum(output_mslt_acm$inc_num_diff_dmt2)
scen_b_ishd_deaths <- sum(output_mslt_acm$mx_num_diff_ishd)
scen_b_copd_deaths <- sum(output_mslt_acm$mx_num_diff_copd)
scen_b_dmt2_deaths <- sum(output_mslt_acm$mx_num_diff_dmt2)

tibble(
  Measure = c(
    "Life years gained (Lx)",
    "HALYs gained (Lwx)",
    "Life expectancy gain (days at age 37)",
    "IHD cases",
    "COPD cases", 
    "DMT2 cases",
    "IHD deaths",
    "COPD deaths",
    "DMT2 deaths"
  ),
  `Scenario A\n(disease mx pathway)` = c(
    fmt_diff(scen_a_ly), fmt_diff(scen_a_lwx), fmt_diff(scen_a_ex),
    fmt_diff(scen_a_ishd_cases), fmt_diff(scen_a_copd_cases), fmt_diff(scen_a_dmt2_cases),
    fmt_diff(scen_a_ishd_deaths), fmt_diff(scen_a_copd_deaths), fmt_diff(scen_a_dmt2_deaths)
  ),
  `Scenario B\n(all-cause PIF)` = c(
    fmt_diff(scen_b_ly), fmt_diff(scen_b_lwx), fmt_diff(scen_b_ex),
    fmt_diff(scen_b_ishd_cases), fmt_diff(scen_b_copd_cases), fmt_diff(scen_b_dmt2_cases),
    fmt_diff(scen_b_ishd_deaths), fmt_diff(scen_b_copd_deaths), fmt_diff(scen_b_dmt2_deaths)
  )
) |> 
  knitr::kable(booktabs = TRUE, align = "lcc", caption = "MSLT results comparison. Positive values = cases/deaths prevented; negative = cases/deaths increased.")
```

```{r}
#| label: plot-mslt-results
#| fig-cap: "Left: life years and health-adjusted life years gained at each age — Scenario A (disease mortality pathway) vs Scenario B (all-cause PIF). Right: disease incidence and mortality changes (identical for both scenarios as they share the same scenario_inc)."

# ── Life years and HALYs comparison: both MSLT scenarios ───────────────────────
ly_compare <- bind_rows(
  output_mslt_dis %>%
    transmute(age, sex,
              measure = "Lx (disease mx pathway)",    value = Lx_diff),
  output_mslt_dis %>%
    transmute(age, sex,
              measure = "Lwx (disease mx pathway)",   value = Lwx_diff),
  output_mslt_acm %>%
    transmute(age, sex,
              measure = "Lx (all-cause PIF)",         value = Lx_diff),
  output_mslt_acm %>%
    transmute(age, sex,
              measure = "Lwx (all-cause PIF)",        value = Lwx_diff)
)

p_ly <- ggplot(ly_compare, aes(x = age, y = value, colour = measure)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(37, 98), breaks = seq(37, 98, 10), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c(
    "Lx (disease mx pathway)"  = "#2563EB",
    "Lwx (disease mx pathway)" = "#0D9488",
    "Lx (all-cause PIF)"       = "#DC2626",
    "Lwx (all-cause PIF)"      = "#D97706"
  )) +
  labs(title = "Life years and HALYs: disease pathway vs all-cause PIF",
       x = "Age", y = "Difference (scenario − reference)", colour = NULL) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 11), axis.title = element_text(size = 11),
        legend.position = "bottom")

# ── Mortality (mx) comparison: both MSLT scenarios ────────────────────────────
mx_compare <- bind_rows(
  output_mslt_dis %>%
    transmute(age, sex,
              measure = "mx (disease mx pathway)",    value = mx_diff),
  output_mslt_acm %>%
    transmute(age, sex,
              measure = "mx (all-cause PIF)",          value = mx_diff)
)

p_mx <- ggplot(mx_compare, aes(x = age, y = value, colour = measure, linetype = measure)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(37, 98), breaks = seq(37, 98, 10), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c(
    "mx (disease mx pathway)"  = "#7C3AED",
    "mx (all-cause PIF)"       = "#BE185D"
  )) +
  labs(title = "Mortality rate (mx) change: disease pathway vs all-cause PIF",
       x = "Age", y = "Difference in mx (scenario − reference)", colour = NULL, linetype = NULL) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 11), axis.title = element_text(size = 11),
        legend.position = "bottom")

gridExtra::grid.arrange(p_ly, p_mx, ncol = 2)
```

## Comparing all scenarios {#sec-mslt-compare}

```{r}
#| label: mslt-summary-table
#| echo: false

tibble(
  Approach    = c(
    "1. Simple life table — all-cause PIF",
    "2. MSLT — disease PIFs → disease mx → general LT",
    "3. MSLT — disease PIFs + all-cause PIF on mx"
  ),
  `How mx changes` = c(
    "PIF × all-cause mx directly",
    "Incidence ↓ → disease mx ↓ → total mx ↓",
    "Same incidence changes; mx × (1 − pif_acm)"
  ),
  `use_disease_mx` = c("—", "TRUE", "FALSE"),
  `Gain Lx`  = c(
    format(round(gain_ly_pa),                   big.mark = ","),
    format(round(sum(output_mslt_dis$Lx_diff)), big.mark = ","),
    format(round(sum(output_mslt_acm$Lx_diff)), big.mark = ",")
  ),
  `Gain Lwx` = c(
    format(round(gain_hly_pa),                   big.mark = ","),
    format(round(sum(output_mslt_dis$Lwx_diff)), big.mark = ","),
    format(round(sum(output_mslt_acm$Lwx_diff)), big.mark = ",")
  ),
  `IHD cases prevented` = c(
    "—",
    format(round((sum(output_mslt_dis$inc_num_diff_ishd))), big.mark = ","),
    format(round((sum(output_mslt_acm$inc_num_diff_ishd))), big.mark = ",")
  ),
  `COPD cases prevented` = c(
    "—",
    format(round((sum(output_mslt_dis$inc_num_diff_copd))), big.mark = ","),
    format(round((sum(output_mslt_acm$inc_num_diff_copd))), big.mark = ",")
  ),
  `DMT2 cases prevented` = c(
    "—",
    format(round((sum(output_mslt_dis$inc_num_diff_dmt2))), big.mark = ","),
    format(round((sum(output_mslt_acm$inc_num_diff_dmt2))), big.mark = ",")
  ),
  `Competing risks` = c("No", "Yes", "Yes")
) |> knitr::kable()
```

::: callout-tip
## Interpreting the three-way comparison

**Approaches 2 and 3 use identical disease incidence changes**
(`scenario_inc`) so their disease case counts are the same. The
difference is entirely in how the mortality effect reaches the general
life table:

-   **Approach 2** (`use_disease_mx = TRUE`): the mortality reduction is
    mediated through disease — incidence falls, fewer people get
    IHD/DMT2, fewer die from those diseases, total mx falls. The
    life-year gain is bounded by how much IHD and DMT2 mortality
    contributes to all-cause mortality.
-   **Approach 3** (`use_disease_mx = FALSE`): the same incidence
    changes happen (same case counts) but the general LT mx is reduced
    directly by the all-cause PIF. This is equivalent to saying: PA
    reduces mortality through all its pathways (not just the three
    modelled diseases), but we want to keep tracking disease cases
    separately.

If approach 2 and approach 3 give similar life-year totals, it suggests
the disease-mortality pathway accounts for most of the all-cause PIF in
this population. If approach 3 is substantially larger, there are
important PA→mortality pathways beyond the three modelled diseases.
:::

## Exercise — extend to multiple cohorts and diseases {#sec-mslt-exercise}

```{r}
#| label: mslt-exercise
#| eval: false

# Run the full MSLT pipeline for males cohort aged 50-54 and females cohort aged 35-39
# Compare: how do disease-specific gains differ by sex and entry age?

for (sex_i in c("female", "male")) {
  for (age_i in c(37, 52)) {

    age_cat_i <- case_when(age_i == 37 ~ "35-39", age_i == 52 ~ "50-54")

    pif_ishd_i <- pifs_inc %>%
      filter(disease == "coronary_heart_disease",
             sex == sex_i, age_cat == age_cat_i) %>%
      pull(pif)
    pif_dmt2_i <- pifs_inc %>%
      filter(disease == "diabetes",
             sex == sex_i, age_cat == age_cat_i) %>%
      pull(pif)

    out_i <- CalculationModel(
      in_data      = mslt_general,
      in_sex       = sex_i,
      in_mid_age   = age_i,
      mx_trend     = NA,
      in_disease   = c("ishd", "copd", "dmt2"),
      inc_trend    = NA,
      cf_trend     = NA,
      scenario_inc = c(ishd = pif_ishd_i, copd = 0, dmt2 = pif_dmt2_i),
      scenario_cf  = 0
    )

    cat(sprintf(
      "%s aged %d | Lx gain: %s | Lwx gain: %s | IHD cases: %s\n",
      sex_i, age_i,
      format(round(sum(out_i$Lx_diff)),  big.mark = ","),
      format(round(sum(out_i$Lwx_diff)), big.mark = ","),
      format(round(abs(sum(out_i$inc_num_diff_ishd))), big.mark = ",")
    ))
  }
}
```

## Comparison with CRA approach {#sec-cra-comparison}

A Comparative Risk Assessment (CRA) applies PIFs directly to baseline burden
without the multi-state life table dynamics. It estimates effects at a single point in time (year 1), while MSLT captures effects over the full lifetime.

```{r}
#| label: cra-comparison
#| echo: false

baseline_pop <- mslt_general %>% filter(sex == "female", age == 37) %>% pull(population) %>% unique()
inc_ishd_37   <- mslt_general %>% filter(sex == "female", age == 37) %>% pull(incidence_ishd)
inc_dmt2_37   <- mslt_general %>% filter(sex == "female", age == 37) %>% pull(incidence_dmt2)
mx_acm_37     <- mslt_general %>% filter(sex == "female", age == 37) %>% pull(mx)

pif_ishd_3539 <- pif_ishd_by_age$pif_inc_ishd[5]
pif_dmt2_3539 <- pif_dmt2_by_age$pif_inc_dmt2[5]
pif_acm_3539  <- pif_acm_by_age$pif_mort[5]

ex_37 <- lt_ref %>% filter(age == 37) %>% pull(ex)

cra_ishd_cases  <- baseline_pop * inc_ishd_37 * pif_ishd_3539
cra_dmt2_cases  <- baseline_pop * inc_dmt2_37 * pif_dmt2_3539
cra_mx_acm      <- baseline_pop * mx_acm_37 * pif_acm_3539
cra_ly_gained   <- cra_mx_acm * ex_37

mslt_dis_ishd_cases_y1 <- output_mslt_dis$inc_num_diff_ishd[1]
mslt_dis_copd_cases_y1 <- output_mslt_dis$inc_num_diff_copd[1]
mslt_dis_dmt2_cases_y1 <- output_mslt_dis$inc_num_diff_dmt2[1]
mslt_dis_mx_y1         <- output_mslt_dis$mx_num_diff[1]
mslt_dis_ishd_cases_lt <- sum(output_mslt_dis$inc_num_diff_ishd, na.rm = TRUE)
mslt_dis_copd_cases_lt <- sum(output_mslt_dis$inc_num_diff_copd, na.rm = TRUE)
mslt_dis_dmt2_cases_lt <- sum(output_mslt_dis$inc_num_diff_dmt2, na.rm = TRUE)
mslt_dis_mx_lt         <- sum(output_mslt_dis$mx_num_diff, na.rm = TRUE)
mslt_dis_ly_gained     <- sum(output_mslt_dis$Lx_diff)

mslt_acm_ishd_cases_y1 <- output_mslt_acm$inc_num_diff_ishd[1]
mslt_acm_copd_cases_y1 <- output_mslt_acm$inc_num_diff_copd[1]
mslt_acm_dmt2_cases_y1 <- output_mslt_acm$inc_num_diff_dmt2[1]
mslt_acm_mx_y1         <- output_mslt_acm$mx_num_diff[1]
mslt_acm_ishd_cases_lt <- sum(output_mslt_acm$inc_num_diff_ishd, na.rm = TRUE)
mslt_acm_copd_cases_lt <- sum(output_mslt_acm$inc_num_diff_copd, na.rm = TRUE)
mslt_acm_dmt2_cases_lt <- sum(output_mslt_acm$inc_num_diff_dmt2, na.rm = TRUE)
mslt_acm_mx_lt         <- sum(output_mslt_acm$mx_num_diff, na.rm = TRUE)
mslt_acm_ly_gained     <- sum(output_mslt_acm$Lx_diff)
```

```{r}
#| label: cra-vs-mslt-table
#| echo: false

fmt <- function(x) format(round(x), big.mark = ",")

tibble(
  Measure = c("Year 1: IHD cases", "Year 1: COPD cases", "Year 1: DMT2 cases", "Year 1: deaths prevented", 
              "Lifetime: IHD cases", "Lifetime: COPD cases", "Lifetime: DMT2 cases", "Lifetime: deaths prevented", "Lifetime: life years gained"),
  CRA = c(fmt(cra_ishd_cases), "—", fmt(cra_dmt2_cases), fmt(cra_mx_acm), "—", "—", "—", "—", fmt(cra_ly_gained)),
  `MSLT (disease mx)` = c(fmt(mslt_dis_ishd_cases_y1), fmt(mslt_dis_copd_cases_y1), fmt(mslt_dis_dmt2_cases_y1), fmt(mslt_dis_mx_y1),
                          fmt(mslt_dis_ishd_cases_lt), fmt(mslt_dis_copd_cases_lt), fmt(mslt_dis_dmt2_cases_lt), fmt(mslt_dis_mx_lt), fmt(mslt_dis_ly_gained)),
  `MSLT (direct PIF)` = c(fmt(mslt_acm_ishd_cases_y1), fmt(mslt_acm_copd_cases_y1), fmt(mslt_acm_dmt2_cases_y1), fmt(mslt_acm_mx_y1),
                          fmt(mslt_acm_ishd_cases_lt), fmt(mslt_acm_copd_cases_lt), fmt(mslt_acm_dmt2_cases_lt), fmt(mslt_acm_mx_lt), fmt(mslt_acm_ly_gained))
) |> 
  knitr::kable(booktabs = TRUE, align = "lccc", caption = "CRA vs MSLT: Year 1 and lifetime effects")
```

- **CRA**: deaths prevented in year 1 × remaining life expectancy = lifetime life years
- **MSLT (disease mx)**: incidence ↓ → disease mx ↓ → total mx ↓  
- **MSLT (direct PIF)**: same incidence changes; mx × (1 − pif_acm)
